summary.intent: Address Codex automated review findings on PR #684 (fogodev/ars-ui) for the Collapsible component, fix the flagged issues, and get the PR through a clean review pass.
summary.outcome: Two initial Codex threads were resolved with commit 78ae9e54, then four additional Codex threads from a second review pass were addressed with regression tests and implementation fixes committed and pushed, covering controlled-to-uncontrolled state transition, id stability, and repeat keydown filtering.

repo: Collapsible component uses a Bindable internal fallback pattern; SyncControlledOpen must update the fallback before syncing the controlled value to preserve state when leaving controlled mode.
on_trigger_keydown must return true only when it handled Enter/Space to give adapters a default-prevention signal and avoid duplicate native button click toggles.
The repo enforces id stability post-init as a contract (similar pattern exists in checkbox, field, and portal components).
Repeated activation keydowns (key repeat events) must be filtered in the collapsible trigger handler.
The project uses cargo xtask spec validate for spec conformance checking alongside proptest state machines and llvm-cov for coverage.
Spec files are co-located across three targets: spec/components, spec/leptos-components, and spec/dioxus-components.

code.finding: SyncControlledOpen logic; needed to update Bindable internal fallback before syncing controlled value to fix controlled-exit state leak.
id caching location; required adding a stability assertion to reject post-init id changes.
on_trigger_keydown; needed repeat-key filtering and correct boolean return for handled keys.
Spec sections covering controlled/uncontrolled transition and keyboard activation contracts; updated to match new implementation behavior.

workflow: TDD sequence was followed: regression tests were added and confirmed failing before patching the implementation.
Codex review is triggered via @codex review comment on the PR and monitored by polling reactions (👀 emoji) and unresolved threads via GitHub GraphQL API.
Review threads are resolved via GitHub GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutation after posting fix references as inline comment replies.
Existing tests that encoded old (incorrect) behavior needed to be updated when the contract changed, not just new tests added.
Verification suite order: cargo fmt check → focused unit tests → spec-conformance tests → proptest state machines → spec validate → clippy → llvm-cov coverage summary.

friction: git commit hung without output during the first commit, requiring process inspection (ps) to diagnose whether a hook or GPG signing step was stalled.
Codex review polling required multiple 120-second sleep-poll cycles (10+ iterations) before the second review pass completed, making the loop very slow to resolve.
One existing test encoded the old incorrect controlled-exit behavior and caused a test failure after the fix, requiring a separate patch to update the test expectation.
write_stdin calls appear repeatedly with no visible output, suggesting terminal interaction or stdin-feeding to a blocked process added noise and uncertainty throughout the session.
open_items: Codex review pass result not confirmed in transcript — unclear whether the second @codex review pass after the fix commit returned a clean approval or further findings.

spec/leptos-components and spec/dioxus-components collapsible specs were patched but the transcript does not confirm whether Enter/Space keyboard spec language was already present or needed adding in those files.
llvm-cov coverage summary result is not shown in the transcript — coverage threshold compliance is unconfirmed.
